research_gaps: Whitepaper is a PDF hosted on CDN -- could not be parsed directly via WebFetch. Architecture and tokenomics details reconstructed from official documentation pages, blog posts, and secondary sources
Vanascan.io (block explorer) is JavaScript-rendered -- on-chain metrics like total transactions, active validators, and address counts could not be extracted via WebFetch
CoinGecko returned 403 for the web page -- market data sourced from CoinGecko API and CryptoRank instead
CoinMarketCap page is JavaScript-dependent -- market data not extractable via WebFetch
DeFiLlama returned 403 -- DataDex TVL ($984K) sourced from search snippets referencing DeFiLlama
Exact number of active L1 validators on mainnet not publicly disclosed -- docs state validators are currently run by professional node operators (PoSA) with planned transition to permissionless
Exact number of active Satya validators (TEE data validators) not available from docs or explorer
No HuggingFace presence found for Vana -- confirmed by prior research agent
Telegram community member count not verifiable -- official Telegram group exists (@Vana_Offcial_Chat) but member count not extractable
X (Twitter) follower count for @withvana sourced from search snippets (286K for @vana handle) -- JavaScript-rendered page not directly verifiable
Exact staking APY is dynamic and approximate -- docs reference ~6% base rate for data validator staking, with variability based on total staked and validator count
Bug bounty programme listed on ecosystem page as Immunefi integration but no live Vana programme found on Immunefi -- may be forthcoming
Corsali Inc (dba Open Data Labs) corporate registration details (state, date) not independently verified -- entity name confirmed from opendatalabs.xyz
Annual revenue figures for the protocol not publicly disclosed
Exact daily transaction count (1.7M claimed May 2025) not independently verifiable from block explorer due to JS rendering

identity.name: Vana
identity.subcategory: User-owned data and data sovereignty for AI
identity.tagline: An open protocol for portable, user-owned data
identity.one_liner: A decentralised data ownership network where users contribute personal data to DataDAOs, earn proportional rewards, and collectively train AI models -- all on an EVM-compatible L1 blockchain with privacy-preserving validation via Trusted Execution Environments.

founding_team.name: Anna Kazlauskas
founding_team.background: MIT '19 (Economics). Former engineer at Celo (blockchain company). Started in crypto via MIT Bitcoin Club in 2015. Previously worked as a policy advisor in East Timor. Co-created Vana as an MIT Media Lab class project in the 'Emergent Ventures' course taught by Ramesh Raskar. Filipino heritage.

founding_team.name: Art Abal
founding_team.background: Harvard graduate. Former data collection lead at Appen. Met Anna Kazlauskas in MIT Media Lab 'Emergent Ventures' class. Filipino heritage. Co-founded Vana in 2021.

team.entity_name: Corsali Inc dba Open Data Labs (core developer); Vana Foundation (protocol steward)
team.entity_jurisdiction: Corsali Inc / Open Data Labs -- San Francisco, USA (state of incorporation not verified). Vana Foundation -- Cayman Islands (registered c/o Highvern Cayman Limited, Elgin Court, Grand Cayman, KY1-1106).
notable_backers: YZi Labs (prev. Binance Labs) -- strategic investment (February 2025), CZ joined as advisor

technical.architecture_summary: Vana operates a two-layer architecture on an EVM-compatible Proof-of-Stake L1 blockchain. The Data Liquidity Layer validates, tokenises, and facilitates data trading through DataDAOs (decentralised data pools). The Data Portability Layer ensures cross-application data sharing while maintaining user control via Personal Servers -- user-controlled nodes that store plaintext data and serve it to authorised parties through grant-enforced APIs. Users encrypt and sign data contributions with keys they control (OpenPGP password-based encryption with HKDF-SHA256 key derivation). Data validation occurs through the Satya Network -- a group of Trusted Execution Environment (TEE) nodes running Intel TDX hardware that execute Proof-of-Contribution functions. Each DataDAO implements its own PoC function tailored to its data type, validating authenticity, ownership, quality, and uniqueness. On-chain smart contracts (DataPortabilityPermissions, DataPortabilityServers, DataPortabilityGrantees, DataRegistry) manage consent, permissions, server registration, and file records. The Data Portability RPC Gateway provides a caching layer for low-latency reads with eventual consistency to the chain.
technical.consensus_mechanism: Proof-of-Stake (PoS). L1 validators stake VANA tokens (max effective balance 35,000 VANA) for block production, transaction validation, and finality. Uses Geth execution client and Prysm beacon chain client. At launch, validators are run by professional node operators (Proof-of-Staked-Authority) with planned transition to permissionless validator set.
technical.what_is_onchain: Server registrations, grant permissions, file records, data schemas, DataDAO registrations, reward distribution, VRC-20 data token contracts, staking contracts, consent and revocation records. All permission grants are enforced on-chain -- nobody including Vana core contributors can modify data permissions.
technical.what_is_offchain: Plaintext user data (stored in Personal Servers -- desktop, cloud MicroVM, or self-hosted). Encrypted data blobs on storage backends (Vana Storage, Google Drive, Dropbox, or IPFS). Proof-of-Contribution computation inside TEEs (Satya validators). Data Portability RPC Gateway caching layer.
key_dependencies: Intel TDX (Trusted Domain Extensions) for Satya validator TEE hardware
Geth (execution layer client) and Prysm (consensus layer) for the PoS chain
EIP-191 and EIP-712 for authentication signatures
Stargate and Hyperlane for cross-chain bridging
The Graph and Goldsky for data indexing

Audit completed. Full report available on GitHub.

tokenomics.token_standard: Native L1 token (also available as ERC-20 via OFT cross-chain bridges)
token_utility: Network security: validator staking to secure the PoS chain
Transaction fees: gas for all data operations, smart contracts, and applications on Vana L1
Data validator staking: staking VANA on Satya validators to earn emissions rewards
Default data currency: primary payment method for DataDAO data access
Trading pair: primary pair for VRC-20 DataDAO tokens on DataDex
Governance: voting on protocol decisions and parameters via Tally and Snapshot

initial_allocation.vesting_schedule: 20.3% unlocked at TGE, remainder unlocked linearly over 36 months with 6-month unlock frequency
initial_allocation.notes: Includes Binance Launchpool allocation (4% / 4.8M VANA) and airdrops. 46.136% of community allocation unlocked at TGE, then 8.977% every 6 months.

initial_allocation.vesting_schedule: 4.8% unlocked at TGE, remainder unlocked over 48 months
initial_allocation.notes: Dedicated to grants and rewards that drive long-term growth of the Vana network.
0% unlocked at TGE. Allocated to the team responsible for protocol development.
0% unlocked at TGE. Allocated to early supporters (Paradigm, Polychain, Coinbase Ventures, etc.).

distribution.distribution_method: Combination of Binance Launchpool (farming), community airdrop, and vesting schedules for contributors and investors. Community + Ecosystem total 66.9% of supply.

emissions.halving_schedule: None -- fixed supply cap of 120M VANA
emissions.next_major_emission_event: Ongoing monthly/biannual token unlocks across community, ecosystem, contributor, and investor categories

staking.staking_type: Non-rebasing position staking (L1 validator staking and data validator staking)
staking.lock_up_period: Subject to unlock/cooldown periods set by staking contracts -- specific durations not disclosed
staking_risks: Cooldown period for unstaking -- specific duration not disclosed
L1 validator staking requires max effective balance of 35,000 VANA
Data validator staking rewards are currently fixed at ~6% APY but planned to become dynamic based on data access fees

tokenomics.token_model_classification: Fixed-supply utility and governance token with structured vesting. 66.9% allocated to community and ecosystem. No inflationary emissions or burn mechanism. Revenue model centres on data access fees paid in VANA.
tokenomics.tokenomics_notes: TGE listing price on Binance was $27.56 (16 Dec 2024). Current price represents a ~95% decline from ATH. The VRC-20 standard (introduced April 2025) replaced direct VANA emissions to DataDAOs with liquidity-focused incentives for compliant data tokens. ICODrops reports 48.11M VANA (40.09%) currently unlocked with 71.77M (59.81%) still locked as of March 2026.

methods.description: Contribute personal data to DataDAOs via the Data Connect desktop app or platform-specific data connectors. Export data from platforms like Reddit, Twitter/X, ChatGPT, LinkedIn, Amazon, and health platforms. Data is validated via Proof-of-Contribution in TEEs and users earn proportional rewards.
methods.minimum_hardware: Desktop computer (for Data Connect app) or mobile device
methods.estimated_returns: Proportional to data contribution quality and volume -- varies by DataDAO
barriers_to_entry: Requires exporting personal data from platforms
Must use Data Connect desktop app or platform-specific integrations

methods.description: Stake VANA tokens via data validator staking at stake.vana.org. Non-rebasing position model where rewards compound automatically. Estimated ~6.18% APY based on recent activity.
methods.estimated_returns: ~6.18% APY (nominal, subject to change based on total staked and validator count)

methods.description: Run an L1 validator node to produce blocks and secure the Vana PoS chain. Requires staking VANA (max effective balance 35,000 VANA). Currently permissioned (PoSA) with planned transition to permissionless.
methods.minimum_hardware: Mainnet: 8-core CPU, 32 GB RAM, 1.2 TB SSD, x86-64 architecture
methods.estimated_returns: VANA token rewards for block production and transaction validation
barriers_to_entry: Currently permissioned -- run by professional node operators

methods.description: Run a Satya validator (TEE data validator) to validate data contributions for DataDAOs using Intel TDX hardware. Process Proof-of-Contribution jobs and earn VANA rewards per validation.
methods.minimum_hardware: Intel TDX-enabled machine (specific specs in Satya documentation)
methods.estimated_returns: VANA tokens per completed validation job plus data contributor fees

methods.description: Build applications on Vana using the Vana Connect SDK, Personal Server APIs, or deploy DataDAOs. The protocol supports builder registration, data access via grants, and integration with the Data Portability RPC Gateway.
methods.estimated_returns: Access to user-owned data for AI applications; potential DataDAO token rewards
barriers_to_entry: Must understand EVM smart contracts and Vana protocol
Requires user grant permissions for data access

methods.description: Participate in Vana governance by voting on protocol proposals via Tally (on-chain) and Snapshot (off-chain). Token holders influence key protocol parameters and DataDAO operations.
methods.estimated_returns: No direct financial returns -- governance influence over protocol direction
barriers_to_entry: Must hold VANA tokens to vote

developer_resources.developer_community_size: Vana Builder Garden Discord with 3,619 members

usage_metrics.revenue_source: Data access fees paid in VANA, transaction fees (gas), DataDAO participation fees
usage_metrics.metrics_source: Vana blog (May 2025), DeFiLlama (DataDex TVL), CoinGecko API
usage_metrics.usage_notes: 1.3M+ users reported on testnet/mainnet (May 2025 blog post). 1.7M daily transactions claimed (May 2025). 300+ DataDAOs established. 16 DataDAOs in active incubation. 12M+ data points onboarded. 140,000 Reddit users in r/datadao. Daily transaction count not independently verifiable from block explorer due to JS rendering. TVL figure is for DataDex only (the DEX on Vana chain).

community.governance_model: Progressive decentralisation. At genesis, Vana Foundation stewards the VANA token and treasury. Governance uses Tally (on-chain) and Snapshot (off-chain) for proposal voting. Phase 3 (12-18 months post-launch) targets transition to full community governance via Vana DAO. DataDAOs have independent governance via their own tokens granting voting rights and profit-sharing. Foundation explicitly states it does not own or control the decentralised Vana Network.
key_community_channels: Vana Builder Garden Discord: discord.gg/vanabuilders (3,619 members)
community.sentiment_summary: Strong narrative around user data ownership and AI sovereignty. Community grew rapidly through Binance Launchpool and airdrop campaigns. The r/datadao community (140K users) demonstrates organic interest in data ownership. Developer community is growing with 83 GitHub repos and dedicated Builder Garden Discord. Token price decline from ATH ($35.23 to $1.45, ~95% drop) has dampened short-term sentiment, though the project continues to ship product updates and attract partnerships.
community.community_notes: Community skews towards data contributors rather than DeFi traders. Strong adoption in Southeast Asia, South America, and Europe. Notable partnership with Flower Labs for user-owned foundation model training.

infrastructure_decentralisation.evidence: Vana L1 currently operates under Proof-of-Staked-Authority (PoSA) where validators are run by professional node operators and reputable institutions selected by the team. This is permissioned infrastructure with a stated plan to transition to permissionless validation. Satya validators (TEE nodes) require specialised Intel TDX hardware, creating a hardware dependency bottleneck. Personal Servers offer three hosting options including self-hosted, but the default is ODL Cloud (Vana-operated). Data storage backends include centralised options (Google Drive, Dropbox) alongside IPFS. Cross-chain bridging relies on Stargate and Hyperlane. The chain uses standard Geth/Prysm clients. Score reflects permissioned validator set with credible decentralisation roadmap but current centralisation vectors.

governance_decentralisation.evidence: Governance infrastructure exists (Tally on-chain and Snapshot off-chain) but the Vana Foundation currently stewards the token and treasury. Progressive decentralisation is planned with Phase 3 (12-18 months) targeting community governance via Vana DAO. The Foundation is a Cayman Islands entity. DataDAOs have independent governance with their own tokens. No evidence of active on-chain governance votes or participation metrics found during research. The Foundation states it does not control the network, but at this stage team influence over protocol direction remains significant. Score reflects functional governance tools with limited community control at present.

token_distribution_fairness.evidence: Community + Ecosystem allocation totals 66.9% of supply (44% community + 22.9% ecosystem), which is a strong community-weighted distribution. Core Contributors receive 18.8% with 5-year vesting (1-year cliff). Investors receive 14.2% with 4-year vesting (1-year cliff). No tokens unlocked for insiders at TGE. However, only 25.67% of supply is currently circulating, meaning most community tokens are still locked. Binance Launchpool distributed 4% (4.8M tokens). The ~95% price decline from ATH suggests early holders who received community allocation at TGE had significant advantage. Score reflects reasonable allocation split with meaningful vesting, offset by low current float.

censorship_resistance.evidence: The protocol design enforces that 'nobody -- not even Vana core contributors -- can modify data permissions; only the data owner' controls access. Permission grants are recorded on-chain and cannot be tampered with. Data revocation takes immediate effect. However, the permissioned validator set means the Foundation could theoretically influence transaction inclusion. Personal Server hosting via ODL Cloud (Vana-operated MicroVMs) creates a censorship vector for users who do not self-host. The Data Connect desktop app acts as the primary interface, creating a client-side dependency. DataDAOs could independently set terms that restrict participation. Score reflects strong architectural censorship resistance offset by current operational centralisation.

data_sovereignty.evidence: Data sovereignty is Vana's core value proposition and the strongest dimension. Users control their data through EVM wallet keys. Data is encrypted with user-derived keys (OpenPGP + HKDF-SHA256) before upload -- Vana infrastructure never sees plaintext. Personal Servers are the 'only protocol component that sees plaintext data' and users can self-host them. Three-layer security: blockchain (permission grants), server (signature validation), and encryption (user-derived keys). Grant/revocation system gives users real-time control over who accesses their data. Data deletion writes permanent tombstones. Storage backend options include self-custody (IPFS, self-hosted). TEE validation means even data validators process data inside secure enclaves. Score reflects industry-leading data sovereignty design with only minor concerns around default cloud hosting.

open_source_transparency.evidence: 83 repositories in the vana-com GitHub organisation under Apache-2.0 and MIT licences. Key repos actively maintained: personal-server (113 stars, 196 commits, updated Mar 2026), vana-smart-contracts (19 stars, 98 commits, Apache-2.0), data-connect (18 stars, 646 commits). Smart contracts audited by both Hashlock (3 audits, all rated 'Secure') and Nethermind (6 audits). Full protocol specification published in documentation. Contract addresses published for mainnet and testnet. Responsible disclosure programme active (dev@vana.org). Immunefi bug bounty listed as ecosystem partner but no live programme found. Treasury transparency not fully documented. Score reflects comprehensive open source with extensive audit coverage, offset by limited treasury visibility.

freedom_score.freedom_summary: Vana scores 59/100 (Grade C -- partially decentralised with significant centralisation vectors). The project excels in data sovereignty (13/15) -- its core value proposition of user-controlled, encrypted, self-custodied data with on-chain permission enforcement is genuinely strong. Open source transparency (13/15) is also excellent with 83 public repos, multiple audits, and published specifications. However, infrastructure decentralisation (8/20) and governance decentralisation (8/20) are the weakest dimensions due to the permissioned validator set (PoSA), Foundation-controlled treasury, and early-stage governance. The project has a credible progressive decentralisation roadmap but at this stage, the team retains significant control over infrastructure and protocol direction. Token distribution (8/15) is reasonable with 66.9% to community/ecosystem but hampered by low circulating supply (25.67%).

what_works_today: User data sovereignty architecture is genuinely innovative -- on-chain consent enforcement, user-derived encryption keys, and Personal Server model give users real control over their data
1.3M+ users and 300+ DataDAOs demonstrate meaningful adoption beyond typical crypto projects, with organic communities like r/datadao (140K Reddit users)
Strong investor backing (Paradigm, Coinbase Ventures, Polychain, YZi Labs/CZ) provides runway and credibility
Extensive audit coverage (9 audits across Hashlock and Nethermind) shows serious approach to security
VRC-20 token standard for data-backed assets creates a novel financial primitive for data tokenisation
Active development: 83 GitHub repos, multiple repos updated within days of research, new protocol specification recently published
what_is_hype: 1.7M daily transactions claim (May 2025) cannot be independently verified from the block explorer due to JS rendering -- transaction volume may include protocol-level activity rather than organic user transactions
Progressive decentralisation roadmap lacks specific timelines or milestones for permissionless validator transition
Data monetisation returns for individual contributors are likely modest -- the value of individual data points is extremely low and meaningful returns require large-scale collective action
The gap between 300+ DataDAOs established and 16 in active incubation suggests many DataDAOs are dormant or experimental
Token price decline of ~95% from ATH ($35.23 to $1.45) indicates market scepticism about near-term value capture despite strong narrative
key_risks: Permissioned validator set (PoSA) creates a single point of failure -- the Foundation controls which validators operate the chain
Intel TDX hardware dependency for Satya validators creates supply chain risk and limits validator decentralisation
Regulatory risk: user data collection and tokenisation may attract regulatory scrutiny across jurisdictions, particularly under GDPR and similar data protection regimes
Data quality challenge: Proof-of-Contribution must continuously evolve to prevent gaming and ensure contributed data is genuinely valuable for AI training
Competitive landscape: Ocean Protocol, Sahara AI, and Near Intents compete in the data sovereignty space, while centralised data marketplaces (Scale AI, Appen) have stronger enterprise relationships
Low circulating supply (25.67%) with 59.81% still locked means significant sell pressure as tokens unlock over the next 3-4 years
Foundation in Cayman Islands with operating entity (Corsali Inc) in San Francisco creates complex jurisdictional structure
competitive_advantages: Purpose-built L1 chain optimised for data transactions rather than retrofitting data functionality onto existing chains
Personal Server architecture with three hosting options (desktop, cloud, self-hosted) gives genuine user choice
TEE-based data validation (Satya network) provides privacy-preserving computation without requiring complex ZK proof systems
Binance Launchpool listing and CZ advisory relationship provides distribution and credibility advantages
Strong founding team: MIT + Harvard founders with relevant experience (Celo, Appen), credible legal leadership (ex-Coinbase)
competitive_disadvantages: Low TVL ($984K on DataDex) suggests limited DeFi activity on the chain
Permissioned infrastructure contradicts decentralisation narrative in the near term
95% price decline from ATH may deter new participants and reduce staking incentive value
Individual data contribution value is inherently low -- the 'own your data' narrative must translate to meaningful economic returns to sustain user interest
Intel TDX dependency limits geographic distribution of Satya validators
comparable_projects: Ocean Protocol (OCEAN) -- decentralised data exchange and data NFTs
Sahara AI -- decentralised AI data and model platform
Oasis Network (ROSE) -- privacy-preserving blockchain with data tokenisation
Worldcoin (WLD) -- user identity and data ownership (different approach)
The Graph (GRT) -- data indexing partner, complementary rather than competitive
assessment.outlook: Vana has built a technically sophisticated data sovereignty stack with genuine user adoption (1.3M+ users) and strong institutional backing ($25M raised, Paradigm and Coinbase Ventures). The core thesis -- that user-owned data is a new asset class for AI training -- is timely as AI data scarcity increases. However, the project faces a critical execution challenge: transitioning from permissioned (PoSA) to permissionless infrastructure while maintaining the data quality guarantees that make it valuable. The VRC-20 standard and DataFi vision are compelling but untested at scale. The next 12-18 months are pivotal as the governance transition to Vana DAO is scheduled and token unlocks accelerate. If Vana can demonstrate meaningful data monetisation returns for contributors and attract enterprise AI buyers, it could establish itself as the leading data sovereignty protocol. If not, the 'own your data' narrative risks becoming another unfulfilled crypto promise.
assessment.overall_assessment: Vana is one of the most technically complete data sovereignty projects in the DeAI space, with a well-designed encryption and consent architecture that gives users genuine control over their data. The project has achieved notable user traction (1.3M+ users, 300+ DataDAOs) and secured strong institutional backing. However, it currently operates with significant centralisation vectors (permissioned validators, Foundation-controlled treasury) and faces the fundamental challenge of making individual data contributions economically meaningful. The 95% price decline from ATH reflects market uncertainty about the project's ability to capture value. Freedom Score of 59/100 (Grade C) reflects excellent data sovereignty and open source credentials offset by infrastructure and governance centralisation. A project to watch closely as it executes on its decentralisation roadmap.
